title: Data & Insights Analyst
description:
Part Time - 3 Days per weekTrafalgar Entertainment Asia-Pacific is looking for a commercially minded Data & Insights Analyst to help us make smarter decisions through better use of data. This newly created role works across marketing, ticketing, productions and finance, transforming data into meaningful insights that drive audience growth, improve commercial performance and support strategic decision-making. You'll play a key role in helping teams understand what's working, where opportunities exist, and how we can continue to grow and engage our audiences.
Reporting to the General Manager, you'll be responsible for maintaining the integrity and governance of our data, developing dashboards and reports, and delivering insights that support both day-to-day operations and long-term planning. You'll work with a range of systems and data sources, using tools such as Power BI, SQL and HubSpot to turn complex information into clear, actionable recommendations for stakeholders across the organisation.
We're looking for someone who combines strong technical capability with commercial curiosity and excellent communication skills. You should be just as comfortable building reports and analysing trends as you are explaining your findings to non-technical audiences. If you enjoy solving problems, uncovering opportunities in data and want to apply your skills in the dynamic world of live entertainment, we'd love to hear from you. You'll join a small, close-knit team where collaboration is valued, ideas are welcomed and individual contributions make a real impact.
We offer ticketing benefits and access to our employee rewards and discounts program. You'll work in a supportive environment that encourages collaboration, learning and professional growth.
For further information about this role, please refer to the full job pack at theatreroyalsydney.com/careers.
To apply, please email a cover letter addressing the position requirements and your current resume to ***email_hidden*** with the Subject Line ‘Data and Insights Analyst – NAME” by Monday 13 July 2026.
Early applications are encouraged as suitable candidates may be invited to attend an interview prior to the closing date.
Applications are open to those with full working rights in Australia. Trafalgar Entertainment Asia Pacific is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
About Us
Jomablue is a premium enterprise event technology company, trusted by some of the world's leading organisations to deliver complex, high-stakes events.
With over 25 years in the events industry, that heritage shows in everything we build and every event we deliver. Named 'Best Event Management Platform' at the Event Technology Awards and certified as a 'Great Place to Work', the recognition reflects what we have always believed: that the people in our team matter as much as the experiences we create for our customers.
Our customers range from global tech giants to iconic Australian brands, and they come back to us because of two things: the quality of our technology and solutions, and the quality of the people behind it.
We are a close, passionate team of genuine human beings who love what we do. The kind of team where people laugh hard, hold each other to a high standard, and actually want to come to work!
If you want to do meaningful work, at a company that is growing and continues to prove itself . . . this is your chance.
The Role
The Senior Data Analyst reports to the Head of Finance & Operations and is the engine behind the data that powers our events. Every event we deliver runs on customer data, and it rarely arrives clean. This role owns getting that data into and out of the Jomablue platform accurately, quickly, and with confidence. Then building the automation so it happens without manual effort.
You will spend most of your time hands-on with event data: working with event leads to understand each event, preparing and validating customer datasets, and delivering the exports and reporting our customers rely on. Doing the work is only half the role we expect you to systematically automate it, using scripts, our platform APIs, and AI tools, so that what takes days today takes minutes tomorrow.
As the event data engine matures, the remit widens. You will review how teams across Jomablue use data, improve reporting and information flow, automate manual workflows, and help shape our product by feeding recurring data patterns back to the engineering team. This is a foundational hire on our path to becoming a truly data-led organisation.
Beyond the data itself, this role is an internal champion for how we work smarter, leading by example in adopting and building with AI within clear responsible-use boundaries.
You must be comfortable with autonomy. You will be given problems rather than task lists, and trusted to shape the approach and deliver the solution. This is not a reporting role building the clean data, and engineering away the manual work, is the job.
When and Where
This is a full-time role based in our Ultimo, NSW office.
Our workload follows the event calendar, and occasional on-site or out-of-hours support may be required around major events.
Your Duties

Own the event data lifecycle end to end: work with event leads to understand each event, then manage the intake, transformation, import, export, and reporting of customer data.
Ensure data quality throughout, validating and reconciling data at every stage so issues are caught before they reach customers.
Automate the manual: progressively replace spreadsheet-driven processes with scripts, reusable routines, and API-driven workflows.
Improve data visibility across the business through dashboards, reporting, and better information flow between teams.
Identify and deliver automation and process improvements beyond events, extending into workflows across the wider business.
Partner with product & engineering, translating recurring data patterns from customer work into platform features, and supporting product testing with realistic datasets.
Champion AI across Jomablue: work AI-first yourself, help teams adopt AI tools and automations in their workflows, and uphold our responsible-use boundaries, including the protection of customer personal information.
Evaluate new data and AI tooling and recommend what the business should adopt.

Skills and Experience
While we list some skills and experience items below, we are focused on finding the right person who wants to learn.
Essential

5+ years' hands-on experience working with data, owning outcomes end to end.
Advanced Excel and strong SQL.
Python for data work, with code you understand and can maintain.
Practical experience working with REST APIs.
Experience with BI tooling (Power BI, Tableau or similar).
A proven automation track record: a manual process you turned into an automated one.
Rigorous validation habits — you never ship data you haven't verified.
Fluency with AI tools as part of daily work, with the judgement to validate their output.
Clear communication with non-technical stakeholders.

Desirable

Version control (Git) and experience documenting work so others can run it.
Exposure to ETL processes or data engineering in a commercial environment.
Experience with event technology, CRM data (e.g. Salesforce), or other high-volume people-data domains.
Experience with Zapier, Airtable, Wrike, Miro, Claude and Slack.
Previous live event or trade show experience.

The Right Fit
We believe strongly in searching for people that are the right cultural fit for our team. Our values are Communication Excellence, Collaboration & Teamwork, Customer Focus, Problem Solving & Decision Making, and Dynamic Adaptability.
What We Offer

Access to ClassPass to support your health and wellness.
Opportunities for personal development.
Training in key skills such as time management, teamwork, and more.
A dynamic and inclusive work environment where your growth is our priority.
Competitive compensation, benefits, and rewards for your contributions and career progression.
Access to our in-building gym, sauna, and golf simulator.

title: Reporting Analyst
description:
We are transforming our Strategy & Digital Operations division by creating a new, dedicated Reporting & Insights pillar. This initiative combines the expertise of our current analysts with several brand-new roles designed to elevate our strategic impact. We are now hiring for these new positions to complete our team structure. If you want to be part of a proactive, value-driven team that blends established talent with new opportunities, this is your chance to help build our analytical foundation.
As a Reporting Analyst within our Collection Services division, you will play a critical role in delivering high-quality reporting, dashboard development and business intelligence solutions that support operational and strategic decision-making. Working within the Portfolio Performance & Reporting team, you will be responsible for designing, building and maintaining interactive Power BI dashboards, optimising data models, and providing analytical insights to both internal and external stakeholders.
This is an exciting opportunity for a data-driven professional who enjoys turning complex data into compelling visual stories, improving reporting processes and creating scalable solutions that provide a single source of truth across the business.
Key Responsibilities:

Design, develop and maintain interactive Power BI dashboards that provide meaningful insights across operational, client and portfolio performance metrics
Migrate and optimise legacy reporting processes, reducing manual spreadsheet-based reporting
Develop and maintain semantic data models, DAX measures and Power Query transformations
Support the creation of a consistent and reliable reporting environment through robust data modelling and governance practices
Deliver deep-dive analysis, segmentation reviews and performance diagnostics to identify trends and opportunities
Produce operational and client insights that support business performance and strategic decision-making
Respond to ad-hoc reporting and analytical requests from key stakeholders across the organisation
Work closely with Portfolio Analysts and SQL Developers to maintain data quality, consistency and reporting standards.

What We’re Looking For: 

Proven experience in reporting, business intelligence, database analysis or a similar analytical role
Strong SQL skills with proven experience extracting, manipulating and modelling data
Advanced Power BI capability, including dashboard development, DAX and Power Query
Experience building and optimising semantic data models and reporting frameworks
Strong understanding of relational databases and business intelligence architecture
Advanced Excel skills and experience working with large datasets
Demonstrated ability to perform data mining, segmentation analysis and performance reporting
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outcomes in a fast-paced environment
Tertiary qualifications in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Business or a related quantitative discipline highly regarded.

What’s In It for You? 

Opportunity to build innovative reporting and analytics solutions that influence business outcomes
Work with modern BI tools and contribute to the evolution of the organisation's reporting capability
Exposure to senior stakeholders and strategic business initiatives
Be part of a growing Reporting & Insights function with significant opportunity for development
Ongoing professional development and career progression opportunities

We offer a range of benefits, including flexible hybrid working, performance‑based bonuses, wellbeing support through EAP and gym discounts, health insurance benefits, novated leasing options, member rates for car share providers, a relaxed dress code and complimentary breakfast snacks and fresh fruit in the office.
About Us –Credit Corp is Australia's largest provider of sustainable financial services to the credit-impaired consumer segment. We are an ASX-listed company with over 2000 staff in multiple locations in Australia, New Zealand, the USA, and the Philippines. We work closely with regulators to provide sustainable financial solutions with the objective of improving our customers’ circumstances as a pathway to mainstream financial inclusion.
The diversity of our people is core to our ability to innovate, grow and achieve great outcomes for our people, customers, shareholders, and the community. We are committed to our inclusive and diverse workplace through attracting and retaining a workforce that reflects the community. We encourage applications from all culturally diverse backgrounds.
We are committed to improving employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and we encourage applicants from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent to apply.

title: Data Analyst

To be considered for this role, you will need: 

A relevant qualification in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Economics or a related field. 
At least one to two years of relevant experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex datasets. 
Experience using data analysis and visualisation tools. 
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and SQL. Experience preparing reports, dashboards and business insights for senior stakeholders. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously. 
Competitive salary package plus superannuation. 
Opportunity to work with leading organisations across the property, infrastructure and technology sectors. Exposure to high-profile industry events, research and advisory projects. 
Professional development and career growth opportunities. 
Collaborative and innovative work environment.

description:

title: Data Analyst
description:
Data Analyst (12-Month Fixed-Term Contract) | Princess Cruises
📍 Chatswood, Sydney NSW 🏡 Hybrid Working | 🌴 9-Day Fortnight | 🛳️ Cruise Industry Perks
Here's what you'll love:
🌴 9-day fortnight – enjoy a long weekend every second week on full pay
🏡 Hybrid work model – 2 days WFH and 3 days in our modern Chatswood office
⚓ Travel perks – cruise discounts plus exclusive airline and hotel deals
🚉 Amazing Chatswood location – close to transport, gyms, restaurants and shopping
🎉 Fun and social culture with team events, celebrations and recognition programs
🌟 Career growth opportunities within one of the world's largest travel organisations
About the Role
Love turning data into meaningful business insights?
We're looking for an analytical and technically-minded Data Analyst with strong Power BI and SQL experience to join the Commercial Management team at Princess Cruises.
This role has been created to modernise and enhance reporting across our APAC business. You'll become the technical reporting expert, transforming complex datasets into powerful dashboards and reporting solutions that help our Sales, Marketing and Commercial teams make smarter, data-driven decisions.
Working closely with stakeholders across the business, you'll take ownership of our Power BI reporting environment, uncover opportunities to improve reporting capabilities, and build scalable solutions that replace manual processes with insightful, automated dashboards.
You'll join a collaborative Commercial team where your technical expertise will complement experienced commercial analysts, giving you the opportunity to become the go-to reporting specialist across the business.
You'll be involved in:
• Building and enhancing intuitive Power BI dashboards that deliver meaningful commercial insights
• Extracting, transforming and analysing large datasets from our data warehouse using SQL
• Designing scalable reporting solutions that improve visibility and support business decision-making
• Modernising reporting by replacing manual Excel-based processes with dynamic Power BI dashboards
• Partnering with Sales, Marketing and Commercial stakeholders to understand reporting challenges and deliver practical solutions
• Troubleshooting reporting issues while continuously improving dashboard performance and usability
• Supporting CRM reporting and contributing to Salesforce reporting enhancements
• Driving innovation and continuous improvement across the APAC reporting and analytics environment
About You
You'll thrive in this role if you have:
• 3–4 years' experience in a Data Analyst, Business Intelligence or similar analytical role
• Advanced SQL skills and experience working with data warehouses
• Strong experience developing, maintaining and optimising Power BI dashboards and reports
• Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a passion for uncovering insights from data
• Experience supporting commercial business functions such as Sales, Marketing or Revenue Management
• The ability to translate business requirements into practical, user-friendly reporting solutions
• Experience with DAX, with Salesforce or Tableau experience viewed favourably
• A proactive mindset with the confidence to work independently while building strong stakeholder relationships across the business
Ready to Come Aboard?
If you're passionate about data, enjoy solving complex business problems and want to see your work directly influence commercial decisions across a global travel brand, we'd love to hear from you.
Apply now and help shape the future of reporting and analytics at Princess Cruises.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: About UsJomablue is a premium enterprise event technology company, trusted by some of the world's leading organisations to deliver complex, high-stakes events.With over 25 years in the events industry, that heritage shows in everything we build and every event we deliver. Named 'Best Event Management Platform' at the Event Technology Awards and certified as a 'Great Place to Work', the recognition reflects what we have always believed: that the people in our team matter as much as the experiences we create for our customers.Our customers range from global tech giants to iconic Australian brands, and they come back to us because of two things: the quality of our technology and solutions, and the quality of the people behind it.We are a close, passionate team of genuine human beings who love what we do. The kind of team where people laugh hard, hold each other to a high standard, and actually want to come to work!If you want to do meaningful work, at a company that is growing and continues to prove itself . . . this is your chance.The RoleThe Senior Data Analyst reports to the Head of Finance & Operations and is the engine behind the data that powers our events. Every event we deliver runs on customer data, and it rarely arrives clean. This role owns getting that data into and out of the Jomablue platform accurately, quickly, and with confidence. Then building the automation so it happens without manual effort.You will spend most of your time hands-on with event data: working with event leads to understand each event, preparing and validating customer datasets, and delivering the exports and reporting our customers rely on. Doing the work is only half the role we expect you to systematically automate it, using scripts, our platform APIs, and AI tools, so that what takes days today takes minutes tomorrow.As the event data engine matures, the remit widens. Outline how you worked end to end, challenges you overcame and how you ensured successful delivery.Target Question 2:Tell us about a time when you had to present analysis or recommendations that were challenged by stakeholders. How did you handle differing viewpoints, communicate your position, and ultimately influence the outcome?Your application must stand on its own merits, and the completeness and relevance of your application will determine if you proceed through to the selection process.If you require any further information about this opportunity, please contact the hiring managerKedar Mehta via04•• ••• 113.To be eligible to apply for this role, you must meet one of the following statuses:an Australian Citizen;a permanent resident of Australia; ora New Zealand citizen.Prior to commencement, the successful candidate will be required to undergo a rigorous National Police (criminal history) Check and obtain and maintain a Security Clearance as determined by the NSW Police Force.Our commitment to DiversityAt the NSW Police Force, we are proud to be an employer of a diverse range of people. title: Client Insights Analyst - Insurance
description: About UsGallagher Bassett (GB) is Australia and New Zealand’s premier third party claims administrator. We manage claims on behalf of insurers, brokers, government bodies and self-insured organisations. We are part of the leading global risk management and insurance group Arthur J Gallagher & Co.Life at GB is an adventure - often fast paced, unpredictable, challenging and deeply rewarding. It is social and engaging, with our unique and positive culture underpinned by The Gallagher Way – values lived daily by our people.In addition to flexible working options, we also offer supportive learning and career pathways and provide ongoing access to training and career development. We invest in our people through strong succession planning and have a number of mentoring and training programs available.The OpportunityThere are some exciting things happening at GB as we continue through a period of significant transformation of growth. And, we want YOU to be a part of it all!As a Client Insights Analyst, you will join a dedicated team who deliver client insights, performance reporting and administration services to the Sales and Client Services function, contributing to growing and retaining compliant and sustainable business. You will collaborate with teams to utilise your technical analytic and data visualisation skills to support the delivery of the broader Client Insights strategy and initiatives stream.This role provides a unique opportunity to work within a team in a fast-paced work environment and play a pivotal part in contributing to continual improvement of our service offeringsto our clients.Whether you are experienced insights analyst seeking to further your skills in the insurance sector, or looking to make a move into a new industry, we would love to hear from you!Responsibilities:Work with a variety of internal and external stakeholders to help uncover insights and develop analytical data stories;Develop and execute database queries;Validate the accuracy of data outputs;Responsible for the analysis, design, and development of the Client facing data visualisation products assigned to you by Client Insights Lead or Head of Client Insights;Build and gather requirements of data dashboards;Represent GB to clients and other stakeholders, collaborating to understand and formulate solutions to meet both the client and GB’s needs;Collaborate and provide SME advice for the delivery of tender submissions, pitches and retention meetings for key clients and prospects.Experience:Tertiary qualifications in Business, Information Technology, Data and Analytics, Marketing, or other related discipline;The ability to engage with clients and stakeholders in the context of end user reporting products;Demonstrated experience utilising PowerBI or similar tools (i.e. Tableau, Looker) to derive insights, and formulate recommendations to support effective decision making;Understanding of data, statistics and database technologies, specifically relational databases and modelling;Demonstrated experience in relationship management, including the ability to develop relationships with internal and external stakeholders as a trusted advisor;Exceptional interpersonal skills, including the ability to manage a range of stakeholders, and actively listen and respond to changing priorities;Excellent communication skills, including the ability to present to senior stakeholders and effectively articulate the offering of the Client Insights group and the role of Client Insights in wider business strategy;Exceptional time management and organisational skills;A strategic mindset and approach to work;A strong sense of team and collaboration;A resilient attitude.To be considered for this opportunity you must have right to live and work in Australia when applying.Unsolicited agency applicants will not be considered for this position.Gallagher Bassett is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Indigenous Australian applicants are encouraged to apply. As a member of the Diversity Council Australia, we recognise the importance of, and embrace diversity in the workplace. We will make reasonable accommodations to known physical or mental limitations of an otherwise qualified applicant with a disability, unless the accommodation would impose an undue hardship on the operation of our business.

title: Senior Claims Data Analyst
description: Company:MarshDescription:Salary: up to $85,000 base salary plus superFull-time positionLocation: 100 Barangaroo Avenue, Tower One, International Towers Sydney, NSW 2000Marsh Pty LtdAn opportunity has arisen for a Senior Claims Data Analyst to join Marsh, based in Sydney.The Senior Claims Data Analyst is responsible for designing and applying statistical methodologies to analyse complex risk portfolios, financial exposures and claims-related data. It has the objective of generating reliable, evidence-based insights to support strategic decision-making, risk management, and performance optimisation. The role exists to transform raw data into statistically valid analyses, predictive models, and interpretable outputs that quantify trends, uncertainty, and relationships within the business and external clients. This includes defining analytical frameworks, assessing data quality and its limitations, and communicating statistically relevant findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.The position operates with a high degree of autonomy in selecting appropriate statistical techniques it uses and is accountable for ensuring the methodological integrity, reliability, and practical relevance of all analytical outputs.We will count on you to:Engage with clients to define analytical objectives, scope statistical investigations, and translate business problems into statistically valid analytical frameworks.Advise clients on the collection of claims-related data, reviewing current collection practices to ensure key-data points are appropriately mapped and tracked in light of the clients risk profile and business objectives.Design statistical approaches to data collection and study structures ensuring alignment between business questions, sampling approaches, and analytical validity.Communicate complex statistical findings in a clear and structured manner to enable the clients informed decision-making.Translate complex analytical findings to clients through reports, dashboards, and presentations tailored to both technical and non-technical audiences.Act as a subject matter expert in statistical analysis within the team.Required skills, qualifications and experience:A tertiary qualification at Bachelor level or higher in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Applied Finance, or a closely related quantitative discipline with a strong emphasis on data science, statistical theory and methods.Experience in statistical analysis, data modelling, or quantitative analysis roles, with demonstrated application of statistical methodologies to real-world datasets.Proven experience in selecting, applying, and validating statistical models, including regression analysis, hypothesis testing, time series analysis, and distributional modelling.Demonstrated experience in designing analytical approaches, defining data requirements, and assessing data quality, reliability, and limitations.Experience translating statistical findings into actionable insights and communicating complex analytical outcomes to both technical and non-technical stakeholdersExperience with analytical and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, or statistical programming environments (R or Python).Why join our team:We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders.We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities.Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well-being.Application open date 11 June 2026 and applications close on 10 July 2026.Please apply using the link below, applications will only be considered from candidates who have the appropriate approval to work in Australia. Successful applicants will be required to complete a Criminal & Bankruptcy check prior to commencement of employment.Marsh Risk is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information about Marsh Risk, visit marsh.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X.Marsh is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.  Applications will only be considered from candidates who have the appropriate approval to work in Australia. Successful applicants will be required to complete a Criminal & Bankruptcy check prior to commencing of employment.Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.
